meta.description: Self-learning and self-training system. Logs every signal and outcome, adjusts confluence weights, tracks per-symbol/per-strategy stats, and runs training routines — all without external AI.

weightAdjustment.description: Each confluence has a learned multiplier starting at 1.0. It adapts with outcomes.

algorithm.onWin: for each confluence used: learnedWeight = min(1.5, learnedWeight + 0.02)
algorithm.onLoss: for each confluence used: learnedWeight = max(0.5, learnedWeight - 0.03)
algorithm.decay: weekly: move every weight 10% back toward 1.0 to prevent overfitting
algorithm.scope: weights are stored PER SYMBOL and globally; symbol weight = 0.7 x symbolLearned + 0.3 x globalLearned

weightAdjustment.minimumSample: Do not trust adjusted weights until 30+ logged trades for that confluence.

actions: Strategy expectancy < 0 after 30 trades -> flag strategy as UNDERPERFORMING, require manual review, halve its selection priority.
Confluence winRateWhenPresent < 40% after 30 samples -> reduce its base contribution.
Symbol winRate < 35% after 30 trades -> suggest dropping that symbol.

backtestLoop.description: Train on historical candles fetched via realtime-data.json.
steps: 1. Fetch N historical candles for a symbol (e.g. 5000 M5 candles).
2. Replay candle-by-candle through the full analysis pipeline (no lookahead).
3. Record every signal the engine WOULD have given and simulate SL/TP outcomes.
4. Feed simulated outcomes into weightAdjustment with a 0.5 learning-rate factor (live trades teach harder than backtests).
5. Store the backtest report: winRate, expectancy, worst drawdown per strategy.
Track conditional stats, e.g. 'on R_75, after 3 consecutive M5 bearish candles at EMA50, bounce rate = X%'.
Promote a mined pattern into a usable confluence when sample >= 100 and edge >= 60%.
Demote/remove it if live performance drops below 50% over the next 50 samples.

spikeCycleCalibration.description: Continuously refine Boom/Crash spike statistics from live ticks.
steps: Record actual tick counts between spikes per symbol.
Maintain rolling distribution (median, p25, p75) and use it instead of the theoretical average in spike probability scoring.

errorAnalysis.description: After every loss, auto-classify the failure.
errorAnalysis.rule: If one category exceeds 30% of recent losses, surface a coaching alert to the user with the matching rulebook section.

memoryPersistence.storage: Persist learned weights, journal, and mined patterns to the app's database or local storage layer.

files.learned-weights.json: current multipliers per confluence per symbol

memoryPersistence.rule: The brain must load these at startup; if absent, initialize all learned weights to 1.0.
